title: rosa pomifera subsp. resinosa
intro: Rosa pomifera subsp. resinosa, also known as prickly rose, is a deciduous shrub that belongs to the rose family (Rosaceae) and is native to the southeastern United States. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. The health benefits of Rosa pomifera subsp. resinosa include reducing inflammation, promoting wound healing, and supporting immune function, making it a popular ingredient in herbal remedies. Rosa pomifera subsp. resinosa can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or salve, and is often used to treat skin conditions, such as eczema and acne.
